title: User, Agent, and Tool Authority, Least Agency, Dry Runs, and Revocation · Language Clinic: Least Privilege Tools
summary: The model inherits a service account, treats user intent as permission, uses write tools for read tasks, and cannot distinguish allowed, approval-required, or denied actions. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the least privilege tools increment in least-agency authorization decision engine. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Issue task-scoped, resource-scoped, time-bounded capabilities and expose only tools permitted for the current state.
Distinguish authenticated user, affected subject, agent, application, tool, service identity, resource owner, and approver.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example agent engineering team handles case 6899 during a controlled support-operations rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate user, agent, and tool authority with least privilege, effect classes, dry runs, denials, approvals, and revocation; telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable; then introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Issue task-scoped, resource-scoped, time-bounded capabilities and expose only tools permitted for the current state. A defensible solution applies Least Privilege Tools, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to least-agency authorization decision engine.

content.code:
# Worked focus: agentpy-least-privilege-tools — Issue task-scoped, resource-scoped, time-bounded capabilities and expose only tools permitted for the current state.
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-tool-authorization-policy-3
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-tool-authorization-policy-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def worked_agentpy_tool_authorization_policy_3(requested_actions: int = 24, authorized_actions: int = 10, denied_actions: int = 8, approval_actions: int = 6, privilege_leaks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorityworked3",
        "interaction5798",
        "tool3dekrow",
        "stateworked35798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if requested_actions < 1 or authorized_actions < 0 or denied_actions < 0:
        return False, requested_actions, "authorization-count"
    if authorized_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions != requested_actions:
        return False, authorized_actions, "authorization-reconciliation"
    if approval_actions < 1:
        return False, approval_actions, "approval-boundary"
    if privilege_leaks > 0:
        return False, privilege_leaks, "privilege-leak"
    assert authorized_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions == requested_actions and privilege_leaks == 0
    return True, authorized_actions, "least-agency-policy"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python agent decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected model, interaction, schema, tool, authority, state, approval, memory, evaluation, security, telemetry, sandbox, deployment, or recovery case, then state which provider, Gemini, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, identity, storage, browser, accessibility, privacy, load, cost, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.
